Viiu Spangler Khare of BERKES CRANE ROBINSON & SEAL LLP (213-955-1150), appearing for defendant and moving party Resco Holdings LLC.
Jason M. Rose of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs and opposing party Bruce and Vicki Talbert.

RULING: DEFENDANT RESCO HOLDINGS L.L.C.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Resco Holdings L.L.C.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is Denied. Defendant failed to meet its burden of showing that Plaintiff does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain needed evidence that Plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855). Defendant failed to address Steve Berg as a person with knowledge of Defendant's actions at Plaintiff's jobsites, identified in Plaintiff's responses to special interrogatory no. 8. (Kujawski Declaration, Ex. D at 29.) Plaintiff testified working in close proximity to Pullman employees working with gaskets, packing, and welding blankets, which created visible clouds of dust. (Deposition of Bruce Talbert at 282-283, 905-906.) Plaintiff's testimony that he took asbestos classes after his employment at Diablo Canyon in conjunction with his description of the products is circumstantial evidence that the products were asbestos-containing. (Id. at 933.) The sophisticated user defense applies to manufacturers and is therefore inapplicable to Defendant. (Johnson v. Am. Standard, Inc. (2008) 43 Cal. 4th 56, 61, 65.)
